title: The Most Powerful Engine on Earth

beats.narration: Ten thousand nuclear bombs — every single day.
beats.understand: HOOK DIRECTOR + COOL JUDGE: slam the shock number, then GET OFF IT — a held card after ~2s reads as 'okay, fine' and gets scrolled. Keep the line short so the slam cuts fast to the spinning storm. NOT a calm cloud; the shock stat is the scroll-stopper.

beats.narration: And it builds itself — from nothing but warm ocean, water above twenty-six degrees.
beats.understand: the storm from orbit + the one ingredient, warm sea, landed fast

footage.intent: warm tropical ocean under broken cloud

beats.narration: That warm, wet air rushes up. It cools, it rains, and every drop releases heat that pulls up even more — the storm becomes its own engine, feeding on the sea.
beats.understand: the self-feeding heat-engine loop as a LIVING animated diagram — constant motion, non-cloud variety

beats.narration: Ranked by wind, a category five runs past two hundred and fifty.
beats.understand: a FAST, bold wind-scale check — kept short so the bars grow and cut before they can hold

beats.narration: At its heart, the eye — dead calm, ringed by the fastest winds on Earth.
beats.understand: COOL JUDGE: show the WHOLE spinning storm, not a zoomed-in cloud. The sickest way to show a hurricane is the entire spiral turning from the ISS — a thousand-mile pinwheel with the eye at its center. A slow push toward that central eye reveals the whole storm AND lands the 'eye' line. One shot, no cropping to a fragment.

footage.intent: the WHOLE hurricane spinning from the ISS — full spiral, eye at center; a slow push toward the calm eye, the whole storm in frame

beats.narration: Warm water and air — and nothing on Earth can stop it.
beats.understand: PERSPECTIVE DIRECTOR: leave orbit for the payoff. The whole film has watched from space; the thesis lands hardest at HUMAN SCALE — a real street drowned by the surge, the way people actually live it. End on a hard Ken Burns push into the floodwater (motion, not a held card).
beats.text: Nothing on Earth can stop it.

image.title: Hurricane Katrina — a street drowned by storm surge
